title: aita for accepting an insurance settlement against my friends wishes?
text: so about 2 months ago i was in a car accident with a good friend of mine. we have a fwb type of relationship. i'll explain why that's relevant in a minute.  the car was totaled and both of us were injured. he had a concussion and i hurt my ribs pretty bad. the police came and took both of our vitals and our information. so here's where the issue lies... he was buying the car from his ex girlfriend and everything was still in her name. she isn't the most mature person imo and they have problems consistently despite being broken up. as a result he didn't want her to find out i was in the car with him because of our dynamic because she would freak out.  i didn't end up going to the hospital even though i wanted to because i didn't want the bill to get charged to her car insurance. he asked me to lie about why i was hurt so i just didn't go because i was uncomfortable with doing that. a few weeks after the accident the insurance company began calling me asking for a statement. the first time they called i asked him what he wanted me to do. he said, "just be like 'i don't know what you're talking about.'" i was also uncomfortable with that. so i ignored them for awhile. the next time i talked to him about it he asked me to ignore them. i told them i wasn't sure what i was gonna do yet. today they called me again and i finally answered. i gave my statement and they offered me a 1000$ settlement for my injuries which i accepted. i feel like it's important to mention that the money could also really help me right now. as soon as i was done talking to the insurance company i called him and let him know what was going on. he is furious with me now because i gave a statement to the insurance company and now his ex is going to find out i was in the car. my thing though is it's already on record that i was in the accident with him and the police have my information. so i don't think i'm massively fucking him over in any way. and i was actually injured so i don't think he's being fair by asking me to lie or turn down the settlement.  so reddit, aita?

storyboard.content: About 2 months ago I was in a car accident with a good friend of mine. We have a friends-with-benefits type of relationship. The car was totaled and both of us were injured — he had a concussion and I hurt my ribs pretty bad.

He was buying the car from his ex-girlfriend and everything was still in her name. They have problems consistently despite being broken up, and he didn't want her to find out I was in the car because of our dynamic.
He asked me to lie about why I was hurt so the hospital bill wouldn't get charged to his ex's car insurance.
I didn't end up going to the hospital even though I wanted to, because I was uncomfortable lying about my injuries.
When the insurance company started calling me, he told me to say 'I don't know what you're talking about' and then later asked me to just ignore them.
I finally answered the insurance company's call, gave my statement, and accepted a $1,000 settlement for my injuries.
As soon as I was done talking to the insurance company, I called my friend and let him know what was going on.
He is furious with me because now his ex is going to find out I was in the car.

It's already on record that I was in the accident — the police have my information. And I was actually injured, so I don't think he's being fair by asking me to lie or turn down the settlement. The money could also really help me right now.
